name: Jambavan
description: Jambavan, also known as Jambavantha, is a prominent figure in Hindu mythology, revered for his wisdom, strength, and unwavering devotion. He is a bear-like humanoid, often depicted as an elderly and powerful being. Born during the Satya Yuga, Jambavan is considered one of the Chiranjeevis, the immortals destined to live until the end of the current Kalpa. He played a crucial role in the Samudra Manthan, the churning of the ocean, assisting the Devas in obtaining Amrita. Jambavan's most significant contribution comes in the Ramayana, where he serves as a key advisor and strategist in Rama's army. He is instrumental in motivating Hanuman to realize his immense potential and fly across the ocean to Lanka in search of Sita. He also leads the bear army in the battle against Ravana's forces, demonstrating his unparalleled strength and combat skills. Jambavan's wisdom and experience are invaluable to Rama's victory, and he is deeply respected for his loyalty and devotion. His presence symbolizes strength, wisdom, and the importance of self-discovery. Beyond the Ramayana, Jambavan is also featured in the Mahabharata where he battles Krishna over the Syamantaka jewel, ultimately recognizing Krishna's divinity and surrendering both the jewel and his daughter Jambavati in marriage to him. Jambavan’s character serves as a reminder that even the mightiest warriors can benefit from guidance and self-belief.
subtitle: The Wise Bear King and Devotee
achievements: Motivated Hanuman to cross the ocean
Led the bear army in the Ramayana war

keyMyths.title: Motivating Hanuman
keyMyths.description: Jambavan reminds Hanuman of his forgotten powers, enabling him to leap across the ocean to Lanka. This act is crucial in finding Sita and relaying Rama's message.

keyMyths.title: Battle with Krishna
keyMyths.description: Jambavan fought Krishna over the Syamantaka jewel, unaware of Krishna's true identity. After a prolonged battle, Jambavan recognized Krishna's divine nature and surrendered.

keyMyths.title: Role in Samudra Manthan
keyMyths.description: During the churning of the ocean, Jambavan used his strength and wisdom to help the Devas obtain Amrita (nectar of immortality). He played a vital role in the overall process.
